question.label: Quality misses
question.name: Where do you think your work most often misses the bar?
question.description: Names the recurring quality gap in a way that can lead to a concrete calibration discussion.

answer: A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.

assessment.note: Names a missing feedback structure — a tighter loop on one skill at a time — which is a deficiency-as-request signaling strong clarity gap and mild growth blockage.

new_queue.label: Working definition
new_queue.name: How do you decide when a design is ready to ship?

new_queue.label: Next two weeks
new_queue.name: Given this, where do you want to focus first over the next two weeks?

history.answer_excerpt: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.

Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.
I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.
A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.
